name: Selenium
contraindications: Avoid chronic intake above upper limit
Non-deficiency high-dose use may add risk without clear benefit

sources.title: Selenium Fact Sheet for Health Professionals (NIH ODS)

description: Selenium enters selenoproteins; required intake is microgram range and chronic excess can be toxic.
summary: Essential trace mineral for antioxidant and thyroid enzymes; high-dose non-deficiency use is uncertain.
mechanism: Supports glutathione peroxidase/deiodinase selenoproteins including T4→T3 conversion.
safetyNotes: Deficiency and excess are both clinically important.
Upper-limit context is about 400 micrograms/day in adults.
interactions: Potential bleeding-risk context with anticoagulants is discussed in references.
dosage: Adult RDA ~55 micrograms/day; some studies used ~200 micrograms/day.
regulatoryStatus: Essential nutrient with dietary reference intakes.
